artifacts.inspiration_value: high — Multiple mineable patterns: hard-gate state machine, subagent context exclusion rules, AGENTS.md inheritance (never rebuild from scratch), campaign lifecycle artifacts (BACKLOG.md, assessment.md, next-round-brief.md), and the efficiency observer's three-dimension model.
artifacts.uniqueness: rare — The combination of profile-scoped AGENTS.md, hard gates with anti-rationalization tables, and loop meta-learning capture is not found as a complete system anywhere else in the evaluated corpus.
artifacts.composability: calls-others — Invokes delivery-review, architectural-review, code-quality-audit, and other skills as sub-systems at named lifecycle gates.
artifacts.description_craft: trigger — Description covers four modes, three profiles, and twelve trigger phrases; the
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Subagents are defined by what they DON'T receive — task agents get task text + AGENTS.md only.
artifacts.notes: The "deliberate context exclusion" principle (agents receive only task scope + AGENTS.md; no plan, no sibling results, no prior wave outcomes) is the single most transferable architectural insight in the stage 2 batch. It solves a real multi-agent integration failure mode: agents compensating for ea

artifacts.intent: Mines Claude Code session transcripts (JSONL files) for recurring patterns and promotes them into skill candidates via a friction × frequency × cost scoring model, with decay over time.
artifacts.adoption_fit: strong — Already in David's stack. The friction × frequency × cost scoring model and the candidate decay system are uniquely his design. Highly relevant to stage 10 (observability of the harness itself).
artifacts.uniqueness: rare — Session-transcript mining with friction-weighted scoring and decay is not a common pattern. Most self-improvement skills capture in-session only; this adds a batch retrospective mode with statistical reasoning.
artifacts.composability: calls-others — Log mode integrates with AngelEye (live ingestion) and promotion feeds into skill-creator. Embedded in a larger pipeline.
artifacts.description_craft: trigger — Description is detailed and contains all trigger phrases across both modes. Slightly long but justified by the two-mode design.
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Friction > frequency. Pure repetition counts are weak signal. The same question asked twice is just normal work; a question that triggered a correction twice is a skill-shaped hole.
artifacts.notes: The friction-over-frequency principle is directly applicable to David's stage 10 (observability gap identification): the places where the harness causes corrections, dead ends, or high cost are where new skills are needed — not places where tasks are simply repeated. The decay model (60/120/180-day

artifacts.intent: Maintains `docs/solutions/` by reviewing stale learning docs against the current codebase and applying Keep/Update/Consolidate/Replace/Delete classifications with headless mode for automation.
artifacts.adoption_fit: mid — The skill is tightly coupled to the compound-engineering docs/solutions/ structure and workflow. The maintenance model (five outcomes + Phase 1.75 document-set analysis) is fully portable. The headless mode and discoverability check are directly applicable to David's brains system. The platform-agnostic question-tool design (AskUserQuestion in Claude Code, request_user_input in Codex, etc.) shows cross-harness awareness.
artifacts.uniqueness: rare — Five-outcome maintenance model with document-set-level analysis and inbound-link graph traversal is not common in knowledge-maintenance skills. Most maintenance skills check for freshness only.
artifacts.composability: calls-others — Calls investigation subagents and replacement subagents. Can be called by ce-compound after writing a new learning.
artifacts.description_craft: trigger — Description covers the trigger cases well. The headless mode flag in argument-hint is load-bearing for automation use.
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Two docs covering the same ground will eventually drift apart and contradict each other — that is worse than a slightly longer single doc.
artifacts.notes: The document-set analysis layer (Phase 1.75) is the most portable design idea: before classifying individual documents, step back and ask whether the set as a whole is well-designed. For David's brains, this maps directly: multiple INDEX.md files across different brains can accumulate contradictory

artifacts.intent: Routes browser automation tasks to the right tool (Playwright MCP, agent-browser, Chrome DevTools, Chrome Extension) based on session length and context, with a HITL pause protocol.
artifacts.adoption_fit: mid
artifacts.uniqueness: uncommon
artifacts.composability: calls-others
artifacts.description_craft: trigger
artifacts.mineable_phrase: David watches. You drive. Pick the right tool, then operate it.
artifacts.notes: The "Snapshot before screenshot" rule (use accessibility tree first, screenshots only when visual confirmation needed) encodes a cost-aware practice that goes beyond most browser-automation skills. The HITL pause format is mineable verbatim. The headed-always rule is a strong example of encoding ope

artifacts.intent: Adds x402 HTTP payment execution to AI agents with per-task/session budget caps, allowlisted recipients, non-custodial ERC-4337 wallets, and MCP tool integration.
artifacts.adoption_fit: weak — Tightly coupled to blockchain infrastructure (Base, X Layer, ERC-4337); only relevant if building agent-to-agent payment flows or paid API access
artifacts.inspiration_value: mid — The spending controls model (per-task budget + allowlisted recipients set by orchestrator, not agent) is a clean autonomy-limiting pattern applicable beyond payments
artifacts.uniqueness: rare — Agent-native payment execution with policy-gated budgets is genuinely novel in the skills catalog space
artifacts.composability: standalone — MCP server integration; no skill dependencies, but requires external wallet infrastructure
artifacts.description_craft: trigger —
artifacts.mineable_phrase: Set budgets before delegation: When spawning sub-agents, attach a SpendingPolicy via your orchestration layer. Never give an agent unlimited spend.
artifacts.notes: The orchestrator-sets-policy-before-delegation pattern (not the agent itself) is the extractable governance idea for dark-factory: any high-privilege capability should be policy-bound at spawn time, not at execution time. The fail-closed preToolCheck implementation is production-grade. Adoption is n

artifacts.intent: CLI-backed skill that resolves brain names via 4-tier matching (exact → alias → substring → tag) and outputs file paths for piping into llm_context.
artifacts.adoption_fit: mid
artifacts.uniqueness: uncommon
artifacts.composability: calls-others

artifacts.mineable_phrase: File discovery tool — returns paths for piping into llm_context. Different from focus (orientation) and brain-librarian (curation).
artifacts.notes: The role differentiation between brain-query (find), llm-context (assemble), focus (orient), and brain-bridge (write-back) is an unusually disciplined single-responsibility decomposition — each skill has one verb. That four-role pattern is worth capturing in any unified context-management skill desi
